mnemonics.title: 7 Steps of IoT Design: PPDISLF
mnemonics.content: **P**urpose, **P**rocess, **D**omain Model, **I**nformation Model, **S**ervice, **L**evel, **F**unctional View. Think: 'Please Plan Designs In Sequence, Levels First'.

mnemonics.title: Domain vs Information Model
mnemonics.content: **D**omain = **D**rawing relationships (entities, UML). **I**nformation = **I**nner data details (schema, JSON format, semantics). Domain is WHO, Information is WHAT they exchange.

mnemonics.title: IoT Levels 1-6 Spectrum
mnemonics.content: **L1-L2** = **S**ingle device (Simple). **L3-L4** = **M**ulti-device with fog/edge (Medium). **L5-L6** = **C**loud coordinated WAN (Complex). Think: SMC for Simple, Medium, Complex.

mnemonics.title: Python Across IoT Stack
mnemonics.content: **M**icroPython (devices), **P**i scripts (gateways), **F**lask/Django (cloud), **P**andas (analytics). Python spans the entire IoT stack from edge to cloud.

mnemonics.title: Functional View = Implementation
mnemonics.content: Step 7 is where abstract becomes concrete: **H**ardware (RPi, ESP32), **P**rotocols (MQTT, CoAP), **C**loud (AWS IoT), **D**atabase (InfluxDB). Think: HPCD for the blueprint.
